<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[arz:كونجوشو جى]][[commons:Category:Kongōshō-ji]][[en:Kongōshō-ji]][[fr:Kongōshō-ji]][[ja:金剛證寺]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[arz:كونجوشو جى]][[commons:Category:Kongōshō-ji]][[en:Kongōshō-ji]][[fr:Kongōshō-ji]][[ja:金剛證寺]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[arz:كونجوشو جى]][[sn:commons:Category:Kongōshō-ji]][[en:Kongōshō-ji]][[fr:Kongōshō-ji]][[ja:金剛證寺]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Kongoshoji(Mie) 07.JPG|religious_affiliation={{ill|Buddhism|WD=Q748}}|prefecture={{ill|Ise|WD=Q328067}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.4575|N|136.78555556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|founded_by={{ill|Kūkai|WD=Q222227}}|deity={{ill|Ākāśagarbha|WD=Q416174}}|part_of={{ill|Q11379122|WD=Q11379122}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q3198670]] Wikidata name: Kongōshō-ji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q3198670]] Wikidata name: Kongōshō-ji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Rinzai Zen Buddhist temple, Japan}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kongōshō-ji | native_name = 金剛證寺 | image = Kongoshoji(Mie) 06.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kongōshō-ji Main Hall | map_type = Japan Mie Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kongōshō-ji | location = 548 Dake, Asamacho Ise-shi, Mie-ken | coordinates = {{coord|34|27|27|N|136|47|08|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=inline, title}} | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]] | rite = [[:en:Rinzai school|Rinzai school]] | deity = [[:en:Ākāśagarbha|Kokūzō Bosatsu]] | country = {{flagicon|Japan}} [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | functional_status = | website = | founded_by =c. [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] | year_completed = c.mid-6th century | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
{{nihongo| '''Kongōshō-ji'''|金剛證寺|}} is a [[:en:Rinzai school|Rinzai school]] [[:en:Japanese Zen|Zen]] [[:en:Buddhist temples in Japan|Buddhist temple]] in the Asamacho neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. Its [[honzon|main image]] is a statue of [[:en:Ākāśagarbha|Kokūzō Bosatsu]]. Founded in the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]], it has been closely associated with the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] throughout its history and contains numerous [[:en:National Treasure of Japan|National Treasures]] and [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Properties]]. The [[:en:Sutra mound|Sutra mound]]s found on Mount Asama behind the temple were designated a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1936.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210420|title=本居宣長墓（樹敬寺）附 本居春庭墓|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=20 August 2020}}</ref>
==History== The early history of Kongōshō-ji is uncertain. The temple claims that it was founded by [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]], who ordered the monk Kyōtai to build a chapel on this site in the middle of the 6th century. Later, [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]] converted this chapel into a ''[[:en:Dojo|Dojo]]'' for the teaching of [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon]] esoteric Buddhism in 825 AD. As Kongōshō-ji is located to the northeast of the Ise Grand Shrine, it was regarded as the protector of the spiritually vulnerable northeastern direction from the shrine.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.iseshimaskyline.com/kongoushouji.htm |title=Kongōshō-ji |publisher=Mie Prefecture Tourism Development Company |accessdate=10 April 2011 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110122100632/http://www.iseshimaskyline.com/kongoushouji.htm |archive-date=22 January 2011 |url-status=dead }}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bes.or.jp/english/parks/iseshima.html |title=Ise-Shima National Park |publisher=National Parks Foundation |accessdate=11 April 2011 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110611210856/http://www.bes.or.jp/english/parks/iseshima.html |archive-date=11 June 2011 |url-status=dead }}</ref> Furthermore, under the ''[[Shinbutsu-shūgō]]'' doctrines of medieval Japan, Kokūzō Bosatsu was regarded as an [[:en:Avatar|Avatar]] of [[Amaterasu]], the principal ''[[kami]]'' of the Ise Grand Shrine, and thus the temple was regarded as an inner sanctum of that shrine. It attracted many pilgrims and grew to be the largest temple in [[:en:Ise Province|Ise]] and [[:en:Shima Province|Shima Province]]s. The temple went into slow decline in the later [[:en:Heian period|Heian]] and [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]]s, until the priest Togaku Buniku of [[Kenchō-ji]] in [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura]] converted it into a Rinzai Zen temple in 1392. The temple was patronized by the naval commander [[:en:Kuki Yoshitaka|Kuki Yoshitaka]] during the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]], becoming the ''[[:en:Bodaiji|Bodaiji]]'' of the Kuki clan. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the temple was supported by the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. Its [[:en:Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)|Hondō]] was built by [[:en:Ikeda Terumasa|Ikeda Terumasa]] in 1609, and was restored after a fire in 1701 by Keishōin, the mother of [[:en:Shogun|Shogun]] [[Tokugawa Tsunayoshi]]. It is designated a National Important Cultural Property.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Following the [[Meiji Restoration|Meiji restoration]], the temple continued to draw pilgrims and tourists, especially after the construction of a cable car in 1925 to facilitate the steep climb up the mountain. However, the cable car was scrapped for metal during [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], and visitors declined in numbers, especially after damage sustained in the [[:en:Typhoon Vera|Isewan Typhoon]] of 1959. This situation changed after the opening of the Iseshima Skyline highway in 1964.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery widths="150" heights="110"> file:Kongoshoji(Mie)_06.JPG|Hondō file:Kongōshō-ji (Niomon).jpg|Niō-mon (rebuilt in 1979) file:Kongoshoji-sotoba_03.JPG|Oku-no-in file:Asama-yama(Mie)kyou-duka_01.JPG|Asamayama Sutra Mounds </gallery>
==Sutra mounds== About 40 [[:en:Sutra mound|Sutra mound]]s have been confirmed at the summit of Mount Asakuma. The existence of the mounds was known since the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], but more mounds were found after damage caused by the 1959 Isewan Typhoon. The mounds consist of a chamber built from mountain stones, and many were found to contain artifacts from end of the Heian period. In 1966, the area was designated as a National Historic Site, and many of the excavated items were collectively designated as a National Treasure in 1963. Many of these items are exhibited in the temple museum.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/863 |title=Database of Registered National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=10 April 2011}}</ref> These include bronze sutra containers, mirrors with [[Amitābha|Amida]] and the Amida triad, and ceramics.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://sips03.narahaku.go.jp/exhib/2007toku/shinbutsu/shinbutsu-2_01_e.htm |title=Exhibition - list of entries (No.50) |publisher=[[Nara National Museum]] |accessdate=10 April 2011 |url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110719232853/http://sips03.narahaku.go.jp/exhib/2007toku/shinbutsu/shinbutsu-2_01_e.htm |archivedate=19 July 2011 }}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=9&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000483 |title=Artifacts from the Kyōgamine sutra mound |publisher=[[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]] |accessdate=10 April 2011 |url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110911113647/http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=9&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000483 |archivedate=11 September 2011 }}</ref> The area of funerary monuments where these items were found has been designated an [[:en:List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments|Historic Site]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1525 |title=Database of Registered National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=10 April 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=4&spetype=11&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000489 |title=Mount Asama Kyōzukagun |publisher=[[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]] |accessdate=10 April 2011 |url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110911113729/http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=4&spetype=11&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000489 |archivedate=11 September 2011 }}</ref>
==Buildings== The '''Hondō''' is a seven by six [[:en:Ken (unit)|bay]], ''[[:en:Yosemune-zukuri|Yosemune-zukuri]]'' building with a ''[[hinoki]]'' bark roof. Built in 1609, it is an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1295 |title=Database of Registered National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=10 April 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=1&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000453 |title=Kongōshō-ji Hondō |publisher=[[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]] |accessdate=10 April 2011 |url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110911113234/http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=1&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000453 |archivedate=11 September 2011 }}</ref>
==Cultural Properties== *[[:en:Kuki Yoshitaka|Kuki Yoshitaka]] portrait (1607) ([[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]).<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/1205 |title=Database of Registered National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=10 April 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&speclass=1&spetype=2&dettype=2&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000455 |title=Portrait of Kuki Yoshitaka |publisher=[[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]] |accessdate=10 April 2011 |url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110911113843/http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&speclass=1&spetype=2&dettype=2&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000455 |archivedate=11 September 2011 }}</ref>
*Uhōdōji statue ([[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]) (Important Cultural Property).<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/3099 |title=Database of Registered National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=10 April 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=3&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000457 |title=Uhōdōji |publisher=[[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]] |accessdate=10 April 2011 |url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110911113342/http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=3&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000457 |archivedate=11 September 2011 }}</ref>
*[[:en:Jizō Bosatsu|Jizō Bosatsu]] statue ([[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]]) (Important Cultural Property).<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011366 |title=Database of Registered National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=10 April 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=3&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000941 |title=Jizō Bosatsu |publisher=[[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]] |accessdate=10 April 2011 |url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110911113421/http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=3&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000941 |archivedate=11 September 2011 }}</ref>
* [[Tachi]] (Heian period) (Important Cultural Property).<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6240 |title=Database of Registered National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=10 April 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=4&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000462 |title=Tachi |publisher=[[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]] |accessdate=10 April 2011 |url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110911113539/http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=4&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000462 |archivedate=11 September 2011 }}</ref>
*[[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]] with twin phoenixes (Heian period) (Important Cultural Property).<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/5272 |title=Database of Registered National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=10 April 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=4&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000461 |title=Bronze mirror with twin phoenixes |publisher=[[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]] |accessdate=10 April 2011 |url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110911113439/http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=4&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000461 |archivedate=11 September 2011 }}</ref>
* Artifacts from the Asamayama sutra mounds (1159–1173) ([[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]]).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[arz:كونجوشو جى]][[co:commons:Category:Kongōshō-ji]][[en:Kongōshō-ji]][[fr:Kongōshō-ji]][[ja:金剛證寺]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[arz:كونجوشو جى]][[co:commons:Category:Kongōshō-ji]][[en:Kongōshō-ji]][[fr:Kongōshō-ji]][[ja:金剛證寺]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[arz:كونجوشو جى]][[sn:commons:Category:Kongōshō-ji]][[en:Kongōshō-ji]][[fr:Kongōshō-ji]][[ja:金剛證寺]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Kongoshoji(Mie) 07.JPG|religious_affiliation={{ill|Buddhism|WD=Q748}}|prefecture={{ill|Ise|WD=Q328067}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.4575|N|136.78555556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|founded_by={{ill|Kūkai|WD=Q222227}}|deity={{ill|Ākāśagarbha|WD=Q416174}}|part_of={{ill|Q11379122|WD=Q11379122}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q3198670]] Wikidata name: Kongōshō-ji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q3198670]] Wikidata name: Kongōshō-ji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Rinzai Zen Buddhist temple, Japan}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kongōshō-ji | native_name = 金剛證寺 | image = Kongoshoji(Mie) 06.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kongōshō-ji Main Hall | map_type = Japan Mie Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kongōshō-ji | location = 548 Dake, Asamacho Ise-shi, Mie-ken | coordinates = {{coord|34|27|27|N|136|47|08|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=inline, title}} | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]] | rite = [[:en:Rinzai school|Rinzai school]] | deity = [[:en:Ākāśagarbha|Kokūzō Bosatsu]] | country = {{flagicon|Japan}} [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | functional_status = | website = | founded_by =c. [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] | year_completed = c.mid-6th century | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
{{nihongo| '''Kongōshō-ji'''|金剛證寺|}} is a [[:en:Rinzai school|Rinzai school]] [[:en:Japanese Zen|Zen]] [[:en:Buddhist temples in Japan|Buddhist temple]] in the Asamacho neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. Its [[honzon|main image]] is a statue of [[:en:Ākāśagarbha|Kokūzō Bosatsu]]. Founded in the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]], it has been closely associated with the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] throughout its history and contains numerous [[:en:National Treasure of Japan|National Treasures]] and [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Properties]]. The [[:en:Sutra mound|Sutra mound]]s found on Mount Asama behind the temple were designated a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1936.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210420|title=本居宣長墓（樹敬寺）附 本居春庭墓|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=20 August 2020}}</ref>
==History== The early history of Kongōshō-ji is uncertain. The temple claims that it was founded by [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]], who ordered the monk Kyōtai to build a chapel on this site in the middle of the 6th century. Later, [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]] converted this chapel into a ''[[:en:Dojo|Dojo]]'' for the teaching of [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon]] esoteric Buddhism in 825 AD. As Kongōshō-ji is located to the northeast of the Ise Grand Shrine, it was regarded as the protector of the spiritually vulnerable northeastern direction from the shrine.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.iseshimaskyline.com/kongoushouji.htm |title=Kongōshō-ji |publisher=Mie Prefecture Tourism Development Company |accessdate=10 April 2011 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110122100632/http://www.iseshimaskyline.com/kongoushouji.htm |archive-date=22 January 2011 |url-status=dead }}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bes.or.jp/english/parks/iseshima.html |title=Ise-Shima National Park |publisher=National Parks Foundation |accessdate=11 April 2011 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110611210856/http://www.bes.or.jp/english/parks/iseshima.html |archive-date=11 June 2011 |url-status=dead }}</ref> Furthermore, under the ''[[Shinbutsu-shūgō]]'' doctrines of medieval Japan, Kokūzō Bosatsu was regarded as an [[:en:Avatar|Avatar]] of [[Amaterasu]], the principal ''[[kami]]'' of the Ise Grand Shrine, and thus the temple was regarded as an inner sanctum of that shrine. It attracted many pilgrims and grew to be the largest temple in [[:en:Ise Province|Ise]] and [[:en:Shima Province|Shima Province]]s. The temple went into slow decline in the later [[:en:Heian period|Heian]] and [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]]s, until the priest Togaku Buniku of [[Kenchō-ji]] in [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura]] converted it into a Rinzai Zen temple in 1392. The temple was patronized by the naval commander [[:en:Kuki Yoshitaka|Kuki Yoshitaka]] during the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]], becoming the ''[[:en:Bodaiji|Bodaiji]]'' of the Kuki clan. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the temple was supported by the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. Its [[:en:Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)|Hondō]] was built by [[:en:Ikeda Terumasa|Ikeda Terumasa]] in 1609, and was restored after a fire in 1701 by Keishōin, the mother of [[:en:Shogun|Shogun]] [[Tokugawa Tsunayoshi]]. It is designated a National Important Cultural Property.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Following the [[Meiji Restoration|Meiji restoration]], the temple continued to draw pilgrims and tourists, especially after the construction of a cable car in 1925 to facilitate the steep climb up the mountain. However, the cable car was scrapped for metal during [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], and visitors declined in numbers, especially after damage sustained in the [[:en:Typhoon Vera|Isewan Typhoon]] of 1959. This situation changed after the opening of the Iseshima Skyline highway in 1964.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery widths="150" heights="110"> file:Kongoshoji(Mie)_06.JPG|Hondō file:Kongōshō-ji (Niomon).jpg|Niō-mon (rebuilt in 1979) file:Kongoshoji-sotoba_03.JPG|Oku-no-in file:Asama-yama(Mie)kyou-duka_01.JPG|Asamayama Sutra Mounds </gallery>
==Sutra mounds== About 40 [[:en:Sutra mound|Sutra mound]]s have been confirmed at the summit of Mount Asakuma. The existence of the mounds was known since the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], but more mounds were found after damage caused by the 1959 Isewan Typhoon. The mounds consist of a chamber built from mountain stones, and many were found to contain artifacts from end of the Heian period. In 1966, the area was designated as a National Historic Site, and many of the excavated items were collectively designated as a National Treasure in 1963. Many of these items are exhibited in the temple museum.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/863 |title=Database of Registered National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=10 April 2011}}</ref> These include bronze sutra containers, mirrors with [[Amitābha|Amida]] and the Amida triad, and ceramics.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://sips03.narahaku.go.jp/exhib/2007toku/shinbutsu/shinbutsu-2_01_e.htm |title=Exhibition - list of entries (No.50) |publisher=[[Nara National Museum]] |accessdate=10 April 2011 |url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110719232853/http://sips03.narahaku.go.jp/exhib/2007toku/shinbutsu/shinbutsu-2_01_e.htm |archivedate=19 July 2011 }}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=9&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000483 |title=Artifacts from the Kyōgamine sutra mound |publisher=[[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]] |accessdate=10 April 2011 |url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110911113647/http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=9&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000483 |archivedate=11 September 2011 }}</ref> The area of funerary monuments where these items were found has been designated an [[:en:List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments|Historic Site]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1525 |title=Database of Registered National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=10 April 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=4&spetype=11&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000489 |title=Mount Asama Kyōzukagun |publisher=[[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]] |accessdate=10 April 2011 |url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110911113729/http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=4&spetype=11&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000489 |archivedate=11 September 2011 }}</ref>
==Buildings== The '''Hondō''' is a seven by six [[:en:Ken (unit)|bay]], ''[[:en:Yosemune-zukuri|Yosemune-zukuri]]'' building with a ''[[hinoki]]'' bark roof. Built in 1609, it is an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1295 |title=Database of Registered National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=10 April 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=1&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000453 |title=Kongōshō-ji Hondō |publisher=[[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]] |accessdate=10 April 2011 |url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110911113234/http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=1&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000453 |archivedate=11 September 2011 }}</ref>
==Cultural Properties== *[[:en:Kuki Yoshitaka|Kuki Yoshitaka]] portrait (1607) ([[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]).<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/1205 |title=Database of Registered National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=10 April 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&speclass=1&spetype=2&dettype=2&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000455 |title=Portrait of Kuki Yoshitaka |publisher=[[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]] |accessdate=10 April 2011 |url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110911113843/http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&speclass=1&spetype=2&dettype=2&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000455 |archivedate=11 September 2011 }}</ref>
*Uhōdōji statue ([[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]) (Important Cultural Property).<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/3099 |title=Database of Registered National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=10 April 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=3&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000457 |title=Uhōdōji |publisher=[[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]] |accessdate=10 April 2011 |url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110911113342/http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=3&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000457 |archivedate=11 September 2011 }}</ref>
*[[:en:Jizō Bosatsu|Jizō Bosatsu]] statue ([[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]]) (Important Cultural Property).<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011366 |title=Database of Registered National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=10 April 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=3&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000941 |title=Jizō Bosatsu |publisher=[[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]] |accessdate=10 April 2011 |url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110911113421/http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=3&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000941 |archivedate=11 September 2011 }}</ref>
* [[Tachi]] (Heian period) (Important Cultural Property).<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6240 |title=Database of Registered National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=10 April 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=4&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000462 |title=Tachi |publisher=[[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]] |accessdate=10 April 2011 |url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110911113539/http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=4&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000462 |archivedate=11 September 2011 }}</ref>
*[[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]] with twin phoenixes (Heian period) (Important Cultural Property).<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/5272 |title=Database of Registered National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=10 April 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=4&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000461 |title=Bronze mirror with twin phoenixes |publisher=[[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]] |accessdate=10 April 2011 |url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110911113439/http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=4&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000461 |archivedate=11 September 2011 }}</ref>
* Artifacts from the Asamayama sutra mounds (1159–1173) ([[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]]).

dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kongōshō-ji | native_name = 金剛證寺 | image = Kongoshoji(Mie) 06.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kongōshō-ji Main Hall | map_type = Japan Mie Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kongōshō-ji | location = 548 Dake, Asamacho Ise-shi, Mie-ken | coordinates = {{coord|34|27|27|N|136|47|08|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=inline, title}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Buddhism|en|Buddhism|WD=Q748|lt=Buddhism}} | rite = {{ill|Rinzai school|en|Rinzai school|WD=Q639625|lt=Rinzai school}} | deity = {{ill|Ākāśagarbha|en|Ākāśagarbha|WD=Q416174|lt=Kokūzō Bosatsu}} | country = {{flagicon|Japan}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | functional_status = | website = | founded_by =c. {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|WD=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei}} | year_completed = c.mid-6th century | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
{{nihongo| '''Kongōshō-ji'''|金剛證寺|}} is a {{ill|Rinzai school|en|Rinzai school|WD=Q639625|lt=Rinzai school}} {{ill|Japanese Zen|en|Japanese Zen|WD=Q189424|lt=Zen}} {{ill|Buddhist temples in Japan|en|Buddhist temples in Japan|WD=Q3141900|lt=Buddhist temple}} in the Asamacho neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. Its [[honzon|main image]] is a statue of {{ill|Ākāśagarbha|en|Ākāśagarbha|WD=Q416174|lt=Kokūzō Bosatsu}}. Founded in the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}}, it has been closely associated with the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] throughout its history and contains numerous [[:en:National Treasure of Japan|National Treasures]] and {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Properties}}. The {{ill|Sutra mound|en|Sutra mound|WD=Q7650105|lt=Sutra mound}}s found on Mount Asama behind the temple were designated a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1936.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210420|title=本居宣長墓（樹敬寺）附 本居春庭墓|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=20 August 2020}}</ref>
==History== The early history of Kongōshō-ji is uncertain. The temple claims that it was founded by {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|WD=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei}}, who ordered the monk Kyōtai to build a chapel on this site in the middle of the 6th century. Later, {{ill|Kūkai|en|Kūkai|WD=Q222227|lt=Kūkai}} converted this chapel into a ''{{ill|Dojo|en|Dojo|WD=Q203609|lt=Dojo}}'' for the teaching of {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|WD=Q550182|lt=Shingon}} esoteric Buddhism in 825 AD. As Kongōshō-ji is located to the northeast of the Ise Grand Shrine, it was regarded as the protector of the spiritually vulnerable northeastern direction from the shrine.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.iseshimaskyline.com/kongoushouji.htm |title=Kongōshō-ji |publisher=Mie Prefecture Tourism Development Company |accessdate=10 April 2011 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110122100632/http://www.iseshimaskyline.com/kongoushouji.htm |archive-date=22 January 2011 |url-status=dead }}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bes.or.jp/english/parks/iseshima.html |title=Ise-Shima National Park |publisher=National Parks Foundation |accessdate=11 April 2011 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110611210856/http://www.bes.or.jp/english/parks/iseshima.html |archive-date=11 June 2011 |url-status=dead }}</ref> Furthermore, under the ''[[Shinbutsu-shūgō]]'' doctrines of medieval Japan, Kokūzō Bosatsu was regarded as an {{ill|Avatar|en|Avatar|WD=Q83090|lt=Avatar}} of [[Amaterasu]], the principal ''[[kami]]'' of the Ise Grand Shrine, and thus the temple was regarded as an inner sanctum of that shrine. It attracted many pilgrims and grew to be the largest temple in {{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|WD=Q864166|lt=Ise}} and {{ill|Shima Province|en|Shima Province|WD=Q1053722|lt=Shima Province}}s. The temple went into slow decline in the later {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian}} and {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}}s, until the priest Togaku Buniku of [[Kenchō-ji]] in {{ill|Kamakura|en|Kamakura|WD=Q200267|lt=Kamakura}} converted it into a Rinzai Zen temple in 1392. The temple was patronized by the naval commander {{ill|Kuki Yoshitaka|en|Kuki Yoshitaka|WD=Q921113|lt=Kuki Yoshitaka}} during the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period}}, becoming the ''{{ill|Bodaiji|en|Bodaiji|WD=Q1084697|lt=Bodaiji}}'' of the Kuki clan. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, the temple was supported by the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. Its {{ill|Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)|en|Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)|WD=Q17051413|lt=Hondō}} was built by {{ill|Ikeda Terumasa|en|Ikeda Terumasa|WD=Q1385927|lt=Ikeda Terumasa}} in 1609, and was restored after a fire in 1701 by Keishōin, the mother of {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Shogun}} [[Tokugawa Tsunayoshi]]. It is designated a National Important Cultural Property.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Following the [[Meiji Restoration|Meiji restoration]], the temple continued to draw pilgrims and tourists, especially after the construction of a cable car in 1925 to facilitate the steep climb up the mountain. However, the cable car was scrapped for metal during {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War}}, and visitors declined in numbers, especially after damage sustained in the {{ill|Typhoon Vera|en|Typhoon Vera|WD=Q1060156|lt=Isewan Typhoon}} of 1959. This situation changed after the opening of the Iseshima Skyline highway in 1964.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery widths="150" heights="110"> file:Kongoshoji(Mie)_06.JPG|Hondō file:Kongōshō-ji (Niomon).jpg|Niō-mon (rebuilt in 1979) file:Kongoshoji-sotoba_03.JPG|Oku-no-in file:Asama-yama(Mie)kyou-duka_01.JPG|Asamayama Sutra Mounds </gallery>
==Sutra mounds== About 40 {{ill|Sutra mound|en|Sutra mound|WD=Q7650105|lt=Sutra mound}}s have been confirmed at the summit of Mount Asakuma. The existence of the mounds was known since the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}, but more mounds were found after damage caused by the 1959 Isewan Typhoon. The mounds consist of a chamber built from mountain stones, and many were found to contain artifacts from end of the Heian period. In 1966, the area was designated as a National Historic Site, and many of the excavated items were collectively designated as a National Treasure in 1963. Many of these items are exhibited in the temple museum.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/863 |title=Database of Registered National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=10 April 2011}}</ref> These include bronze sutra containers, mirrors with [[Amitābha|Amida]] and the Amida triad, and ceramics.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://sips03.narahaku.go.jp/exhib/2007toku/shinbutsu/shinbutsu-2_01_e.htm |title=Exhibition - list of entries (No.50) |publisher=[[Nara National Museum]] |accessdate=10 April 2011 |url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110719232853/http://sips03.narahaku.go.jp/exhib/2007toku/shinbutsu/shinbutsu-2_01_e.htm |archivedate=19 July 2011 }}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=9&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000483 |title=Artifacts from the Kyōgamine sutra mound |publisher={{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}} |accessdate=10 April 2011 |url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110911113647/http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=9&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000483 |archivedate=11 September 2011 }}</ref> The area of funerary monuments where these items were found has been designated an {{ill|List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments|en|List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments|WD=Q20982902|lt=Historic Site}}.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1525 |title=Database of Registered National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=10 April 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=4&spetype=11&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000489 |title=Mount Asama Kyōzukagun |publisher={{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}} |accessdate=10 April 2011 |url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110911113729/http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=4&spetype=11&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000489 |archivedate=11 September 2011 }}</ref>
==Buildings== The '''Hondō''' is a seven by six {{ill|Ken (unit)|en|Ken (unit)|WD=Q3195007|lt=bay}}, ''[[:en:Yosemune-zukuri|Yosemune-zukuri]]'' building with a ''[[hinoki]]'' bark roof. Built in 1609, it is an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}}.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1295 |title=Database of Registered National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=10 April 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=1&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000453 |title=Kongōshō-ji Hondō |publisher={{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}} |accessdate=10 April 2011 |url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110911113234/http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=1&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000453 |archivedate=11 September 2011 }}</ref>
==Cultural Properties== *{{ill|Kuki Yoshitaka|en|Kuki Yoshitaka|WD=Q921113|lt=Kuki Yoshitaka}} portrait (1607) ({{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}}).<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/1205 |title=Database of Registered National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=10 April 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&speclass=1&spetype=2&dettype=2&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000455 |title=Portrait of Kuki Yoshitaka |publisher={{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}} |accessdate=10 April 2011 |url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110911113843/http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&speclass=1&spetype=2&dettype=2&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000455 |archivedate=11 September 2011 }}</ref>
*Uhōdōji statue ({{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}) (Important Cultural Property).<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/3099 |title=Database of Registered National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=10 April 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=3&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000457 |title=Uhōdōji |publisher={{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}} |accessdate=10 April 2011 |url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110911113342/http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=3&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000457 |archivedate=11 September 2011 }}</ref>
*[[:en:Jizō Bosatsu|Jizō Bosatsu]] statue ({{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}}) (Important Cultural Property).<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011366 |title=Database of Registered National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=10 April 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=3&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000941 |title=Jizō Bosatsu |publisher={{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}} |accessdate=10 April 2011 |url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110911113421/http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=3&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000941 |archivedate=11 September 2011 }}</ref>
* [[Tachi]] (Heian period) (Important Cultural Property).<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6240 |title=Database of Registered National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=10 April 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=4&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000462 |title=Tachi |publisher={{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}} |accessdate=10 April 2011 |url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110911113539/http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=4&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000462 |archivedate=11 September 2011 }}</ref>
*{{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}} with twin phoenixes (Heian period) (Important Cultural Property).<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/5272 |title=Database of Registered National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=10 April 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=4&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000461 |title=Bronze mirror with twin phoenixes |publisher={{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}} |accessdate=10 April 2011 |url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110911113439/http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=4&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000461 |archivedate=11 September 2011 }}</ref>
* Artifacts from the Asamayama sutra mounds (1159–1173) ({{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|WD=Q1139795|lt=National Treasure}}).

The {{ill|Sutra mound|en|Sutra mound|WD=Q7650105|lt=Sutra mound}}s found on Mount Asama behind the temple were designated a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1936.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210420|title=本居宣長墓（樹敬寺）附 本居春庭墓|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=20 August 2020}}</ref>
==History== The early history of Kongōshō-ji is uncertain. The temple claims that it was founded by {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|WD=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei}}, who ordered the monk Kyōtai to build a chapel on this site in the middle of the 6th century. Later, {{ill|Kūkai|en|Kūkai|WD=Q222227|lt=Kūkai}} converted this chapel into a ''{{ill|Dojo|en|Dojo|WD=Q203609|lt=Dojo}}'' for the teaching of {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|WD=Q550182|lt=Shingon}} esoteric Buddhism in 825 AD. As Kongōshō-ji is located to the northeast of the Ise Grand Shrine, it was regarded as the protector of the spiritually vulnerable northeastern direction from the shrine.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.iseshimaskyline.com/kongoushouji.htm |title=Kongōshō-ji |publisher=Mie Prefecture Tourism Development Company |accessdate=10 April 2011 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110122100632/http://www.iseshimaskyline.com/kongoushouji.htm |archive-date=22 January 2011 |url-status=dead }}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bes.or.jp/english/parks/iseshima.html |title=Ise-Shima National Park |publisher=National Parks Foundation |accessdate=11 April 2011 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110611210856/http://www.bes.or.jp/english/parks/iseshima.html |archive-date=11 June 2011 |url-status=dead }}</ref> Furthermore, under the ''[[Shinbutsu-shūgō]]'' doctrines of medieval Japan, Kokūzō Bosatsu was regarded as an {{ill|Avatar|en|Avatar|WD=Q83090|lt=Avatar}} of [[Amaterasu]], the principal ''[[kami]]'' of the Ise Grand Shrine, and thus the temple was regarded as an inner sanctum of that shrine. It attracted many pilgrims and grew to be the largest temple in {{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|WD=Q864166|lt=Ise}} and {{ill|Shima Province|en|Shima Province|WD=Q1053722|lt=Shima Province}}s. The temple went into slow decline in the later {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian}} and {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}}s, until the priest Togaku Buniku of [[Kenchō-ji]] in {{ill|Kamakura|en|Kamakura|WD=Q200267|lt=Kamakura}} converted it into a Rinzai Zen temple in 1392. The temple was patronized by the naval commander {{ill|Kuki Yoshitaka|en|Kuki Yoshitaka|WD=Q921113|lt=Kuki Yoshitaka}} during the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period}}, becoming the ''{{ill|Bodaiji|en|Bodaiji|WD=Q1084697|lt=Bodaiji}}'' of the Kuki clan. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, the temple was supported by the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. Its {{ill|Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)|en|Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)|WD=Q17051413|lt=Hondō}} was built by {{ill|Ikeda Terumasa|en|Ikeda Terumasa|WD=Q1385927|lt=Ikeda Terumasa}} in 1609, and was restored after a fire in 1701 by Keishōin, the mother of {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Shogun}} [[Tokugawa Tsunayoshi]]. It is designated a National Important Cultural Property.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Following the [[Meiji Restoration|Meiji restoration]], the temple continued to draw pilgrims and tourists, especially after the construction of a cable car in 1925 to facilitate the steep climb up the mountain. However, the cable car was scrapped for metal during {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War}}, and visitors declined in numbers, especially after damage sustained in the {{ill|Typhoon Vera|en|Typhoon Vera|WD=Q1060156|lt=Isewan Typhoon}} of 1959. This situation changed after the opening of the Iseshima Skyline highway in 1964.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery widths="150" heights="110"> file:Kongoshoji(Mie)_06.JPG|Hondō file:Kongōshō-ji (Niomon).jpg|Niō-mon (rebuilt in 1979) file:Kongoshoji-sotoba_03.JPG|Oku-no-in file:Asama-yama(Mie)kyou-duka_01.JPG|Asamayama Sutra Mounds </gallery>
==Sutra mounds== About 40 {{ill|Sutra mound|en|Sutra mound|WD=Q7650105|lt=Sutra mound}}s have been confirmed at the summit of Mount Asakuma. The existence of the mounds was known since the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}, but more mounds were found after damage caused by the 1959 Isewan Typhoon. The mounds consist of a chamber built from mountain stones, and many were found to contain artifacts from end of the Heian period. In 1966, the area was designated as a National Historic Site, and many of the excavated items were collectively designated as a National Treasure in 1963. Many of these items are exhibited in the temple museum.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/863 |title=Database of Registered National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=10 April 2011}}</ref> These include bronze sutra containers, mirrors with [[Amitābha|Amida]] and the Amida triad, and ceramics.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://sips03.narahaku.go.jp/exhib/2007toku/shinbutsu/shinbutsu-2_01_e.htm |title=Exhibition - list of entries (No.50) |publisher=[[Nara National Museum]] |accessdate=10 April 2011 |url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110719232853/http://sips03.narahaku.go.jp/exhib/2007toku/shinbutsu/shinbutsu-2_01_e.htm |archivedate=19 July 2011 }}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=9&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000483 |title=Artifacts from the Kyōgamine sutra mound |publisher={{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}} |accessdate=10 April 2011 |url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110911113647/http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=9&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000483 |archivedate=11 September 2011 }}</ref> The area of funerary monuments where these items were found has been designated an {{ill|List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments|en|List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments|WD=Q20982902|lt=Historic Site}}.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1525 |title=Database of Registered National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=10 April 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=4&spetype=11&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000489 |title=Mount Asama Kyōzukagun |publisher={{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}} |accessdate=10 April 2011 |url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110911113729/http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=4&spetype=11&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000489 |archivedate=11 September 2011 }}</ref>
==Buildings== The '''Hondō''' is a seven by six {{ill|Ken (unit)|en|Ken (unit)|WD=Q3195007|lt=bay}}, ''[[:en:Yosemune-zukuri|Yosemune-zukuri]]'' building with a ''[[hinoki]]'' bark roof. Built in 1609, it is an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}}.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1295 |title=Database of Registered National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=10 April 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=1&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000453 |title=Kongōshō-ji Hondō |publisher={{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}} |accessdate=10 April 2011 |url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110911113234/http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=1&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000453 |archivedate=11 September 2011 }}</ref>
==Cultural Properties== *{{ill|Kuki Yoshitaka|en|Kuki Yoshitaka|WD=Q921113|lt=Kuki Yoshitaka}} portrait (1607) ({{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}}).<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/1205 |title=Database of Registered National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=10 April 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&speclass=1&spetype=2&dettype=2&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000455 |title=Portrait of Kuki Yoshitaka |publisher={{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}} |accessdate=10 April 2011 |url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110911113843/http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&speclass=1&spetype=2&dettype=2&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000455 |archivedate=11 September 2011 }}</ref>
*Uhōdōji statue ({{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}) (Important Cultural Property).<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/3099 |title=Database of Registered National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=10 April 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=3&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000457 |title=Uhōdōji |publisher={{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}} |accessdate=10 April 2011 |url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110911113342/http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=3&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000457 |archivedate=11 September 2011 }}</ref>
*[[:en:Jizō Bosatsu|Jizō Bosatsu]] statue ({{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}}) (Important Cultural Property).<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011366 |title=Database of Registered National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=10 April 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=3&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000941 |title=Jizō Bosatsu |publisher={{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}} |accessdate=10 April 2011 |url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110911113421/http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=3&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000941 |archivedate=11 September 2011 }}</ref>
* [[Tachi]] (Heian period) (Important Cultural Property).<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6240 |title=Database of Registered National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=10 April 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=4&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000462 |title=Tachi |publisher={{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}} |accessdate=10 April 2011 |url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110911113539/http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=4&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000462 |archivedate=11 September 2011 }}</ref>
*{{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}} with twin phoenixes (Heian period) (Important Cultural Property).<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/5272 |title=Database of Registered National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=10 April 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=4&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000461 |title=Bronze mirror with twin phoenixes |publisher={{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}} |accessdate=10 April 2011 |url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110911113439/http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=4&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000461 |archivedate=11 September 2011 }}</ref>
* Artifacts from the Asamayama sutra mounds (1159–1173) ({{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|WD=Q1139795|lt=National Treasure}}).

{{nihongo| '''Kongōshō-ji'''|金剛證寺|}} is a {{ill|Rinzai school|en|Rinzai school|WD=Q639625|lt=Rinzai school}} {{ill|Japanese Zen|en|Japanese Zen|WD=Q189424|lt=Zen}} {{ill|Buddhist temples in Japan|en|Buddhist temples in Japan|WD=Q3141900|lt=Buddhist temple}} in the Asamacho neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. Its [[honzon|main image]] is a statue of {{ill|Ākāśagarbha|en|Ākāśagarbha|WD=Q416174|lt=Kokūzō Bosatsu}}. Founded in the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}}, it has been closely associated with the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] throughout its history and contains numerous [[:en:National Treasure of Japan|National Treasures]] and {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Properties}}. The {{ill|Sutra mound|en|Sutra mound|WD=Q7650105|lt=Sutra mound}}s found on Mount Asama behind the temple were designated a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1936.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210420|title=本居宣長墓（樹敬寺）附 本居春庭墓|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=20 August 2020}}</ref>
==History== The early history of Kongōshō-ji is uncertain. The temple claims that it was founded by {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|WD=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei}}, who ordered the monk Kyōtai to build a chapel on this site in the middle of the 6th century. Later, {{ill|Kūkai|en|Kūkai|WD=Q222227|lt=Kūkai}} converted this chapel into a ''{{ill|Dojo|en|Dojo|WD=Q203609|lt=Dojo}}'' for the teaching of {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|WD=Q550182|lt=Shingon}} esoteric Buddhism in 825 AD. As Kongōshō-ji is located to the northeast of the Ise Grand Shrine, it was regarded as the protector of the spiritually vulnerable northeastern direction from the shrine.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.iseshimaskyline.com/kongoushouji.htm |title=Kongōshō-ji |publisher=Mie Prefecture Tourism Development Company |accessdate=10 April 2011 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110122100632/http://www.iseshimaskyline.com/kongoushouji.htm |archive-date=22 January 2011 |url-status=dead }}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bes.or.jp/english/parks/iseshima.html |title=Ise-Shima National Park |publisher=National Parks Foundation |accessdate=11 April 2011 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110611210856/http://www.bes.or.jp/english/parks/iseshima.html |archive-date=11 June 2011 |url-status=dead }}</ref> Furthermore, under the ''[[Shinbutsu-shūgō]]'' doctrines of medieval Japan, Kokūzō Bosatsu was regarded as an {{ill|Avatar|en|Avatar|WD=Q83090|lt=Avatar}} of [[Amaterasu]], the principal ''[[kami]]'' of the Ise Grand Shrine, and thus the temple was regarded as an inner sanctum of that shrine. It attracted many pilgrims and grew to be the largest temple in {{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|WD=Q864166|lt=Ise}} and {{ill|Shima Province|en|Shima Province|WD=Q1053722|lt=Shima Province}}s. The temple went into slow decline in the later {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian}} and {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}}s, until the priest Togaku Buniku of [[Kenchō-ji]] in {{ill|Kamakura|en|Kamakura|WD=Q200267|lt=Kamakura}} converted it into a Rinzai Zen temple in 1392. The temple was patronized by the naval commander {{ill|Kuki Yoshitaka|en|Kuki Yoshitaka|WD=Q921113|lt=Kuki Yoshitaka}} during the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period}}, becoming the ''{{ill|Bodaiji|en|Bodaiji|WD=Q1084697|lt=Bodaiji}}'' of the Kuki clan. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, the temple was supported by the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. Its {{ill|Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)|en|Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)|WD=Q17051413|lt=Hondō}} was built by {{ill|Ikeda Terumasa|en|Ikeda Terumasa|WD=Q1385927|lt=Ikeda Terumasa}} in 1609, and was restored after a fire in 1701 by Keishōin, the mother of {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Shogun}} [[Tokugawa Tsunayoshi]]. It is designated a National Important Cultural Property.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Following the [[Meiji Restoration|Meiji restoration]], the temple continued to draw pilgrims and tourists, especially after the construction of a cable car in 1925 to facilitate the steep climb up the mountain. However, the cable car was scrapped for metal during {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War}}, and visitors declined in numbers, especially after damage sustained in the {{ill|Typhoon Vera|en|Typhoon Vera|WD=Q1060156|lt=Isewan Typhoon}} of 1959. This situation changed after the opening of the Iseshima Skyline highway in 1964.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery widths="150" heights="110"> file:Kongoshoji(Mie)_06.JPG|Hondō file:Kongōshō-ji (Niomon).jpg|Niō-mon (rebuilt in 1979) file:Kongoshoji-sotoba_03.JPG|Oku-no-in file:Asama-yama(Mie)kyou-duka_01.JPG|Asamayama Sutra Mounds </gallery>
==Sutra mounds== About 40 {{ill|Sutra mound|en|Sutra mound|WD=Q7650105|lt=Sutra mound}}s have been confirmed at the summit of Mount Asakuma. The existence of the mounds was known since the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}, but more mounds were found after damage caused by the 1959 Isewan Typhoon. The mounds consist of a chamber built from mountain stones, and many were found to contain artifacts from end of the Heian period. In 1966, the area was designated as a National Historic Site, and many of the excavated items were collectively designated as a National Treasure in 1963. Many of these items are exhibited in the temple museum.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/863 |title=Database of Registered National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=10 April 2011}}</ref> These include bronze sutra containers, mirrors with [[Amitābha|Amida]] and the Amida triad, and ceramics.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://sips03.narahaku.go.jp/exhib/2007toku/shinbutsu/shinbutsu-2_01_e.htm |title=Exhibition - list of entries (No.50) |publisher=[[Nara National Museum]] |accessdate=10 April 2011 |url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110719232853/http://sips03.narahaku.go.jp/exhib/2007toku/shinbutsu/shinbutsu-2_01_e.htm |archivedate=19 July 2011 }}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=9&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000483 |title=Artifacts from the Kyōgamine sutra mound |publisher={{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}} |accessdate=10 April 2011 |url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110911113647/http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=9&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000483 |archivedate=11 September 2011 }}</ref> The area of funerary monuments where these items were found has been designated an {{ill|List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments|en|List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments|WD=Q20982902|lt=Historic Site}}.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1525 |title=Database of Registered National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=10 April 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=4&spetype=11&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000489 |title=Mount Asama Kyōzukagun |publisher={{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}} |accessdate=10 April 2011 |url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110911113729/http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=4&spetype=11&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000489 |archivedate=11 September 2011 }}</ref>
==Buildings== The '''Hondō''' is a seven by six {{ill|Ken (unit)|en|Ken (unit)|WD=Q3195007|lt=bay}}, ''[[:en:Yosemune-zukuri|Yosemune-zukuri]]'' building with a ''[[hinoki]]'' bark roof. Built in 1609, it is an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}}.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1295 |title=Database of Registered National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=10 April 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=1&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000453 |title=Kongōshō-ji Hondō |publisher={{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}} |accessdate=10 April 2011 |url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110911113234/http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=1&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000453 |archivedate=11 September 2011 }}</ref>
==Cultural Properties== *{{ill|Kuki Yoshitaka|en|Kuki Yoshitaka|WD=Q921113|lt=Kuki Yoshitaka}} portrait (1607) ({{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}}).<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/1205 |title=Database of Registered National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=10 April 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&speclass=1&spetype=2&dettype=2&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000455 |title=Portrait of Kuki Yoshitaka |publisher={{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}} |accessdate=10 April 2011 |url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110911113843/http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&speclass=1&spetype=2&dettype=2&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000455 |archivedate=11 September 2011 }}</ref>
*Uhōdōji statue ({{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}) (Important Cultural Property).<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/3099 |title=Database of Registered National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=10 April 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=3&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000457 |title=Uhōdōji |publisher={{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}} |accessdate=10 April 2011 |url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110911113342/http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=3&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000457 |archivedate=11 September 2011 }}</ref>
*[[:en:Jizō Bosatsu|Jizō Bosatsu]] statue ({{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}}) (Important Cultural Property).<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011366 |title=Database of Registered National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=10 April 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=3&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000941 |title=Jizō Bosatsu |publisher={{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}} |accessdate=10 April 2011 |url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110911113421/http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=3&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000941 |archivedate=11 September 2011 }}</ref>
* [[Tachi]] (Heian period) (Important Cultural Property).<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6240 |title=Database of Registered National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=10 April 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=4&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000462 |title=Tachi |publisher={{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}} |accessdate=10 April 2011 |url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110911113539/http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=4&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000462 |archivedate=11 September 2011 }}</ref>
*{{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}} with twin phoenixes (Heian period) (Important Cultural Property).<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/5272 |title=Database of Registered National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=10 April 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=4&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000461 |title=Bronze mirror with twin phoenixes |publisher={{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}} |accessdate=10 April 2011 |url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110911113439/http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=4&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000461 |archivedate=11 September 2011 }}</ref>
* Artifacts from the Asamayama sutra mounds (1159–1173) ({{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|WD=Q1139795|lt=National Treasure}}).
