<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[arz:كونجوشو جى]][[co:commons:Category:Kongōshō-ji]][[en:Kongōshō-ji]][[fr:Kongōshō-ji]][[ja:金剛證寺]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[arz:كونجوشو جى]][[co:commons:Category:Kongōshō-ji]][[en:Kongōshō-ji]][[fr:Kongōshō-ji]][[ja:金剛證寺]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[arz:كونجوشو جى]][[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Kongōshō-ji]][[en:Kongōshō-ji]][[fr:Kongōshō-ji]][[ja:金剛證寺]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Kongoshoji(Mie) 07.JPG|religious_affiliation={{ill|Buddhism|WD=Q748|1=Buddhism}}|prefecture={{ill|Ise|WD=Q328067|1=Ise, Mie}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.4575|N|136.78555556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|founded_by={{ill|Kūkai|WD=Q222227|1=Kūkai}}|deity={{ill|Ākāśagarbha|WD=Q416174|1=Ākāśagarbha}}|part_of={{ill|Q11379122|WD=Q11379122|1=Q11379122}}}}
{{Short description|Rinzai Zen Buddhist temple, Japan}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kongōshō-ji | native_name = 金剛證寺 | image = Kongoshoji(Mie) 06.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kongōshō-ji Main Hall | map_type = Japan Mie Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kongōshō-ji | location = 548 Dake, Asamacho Ise-shi, Mie-ken | coordinates = {{coord|34|27|27|N|136|47|08|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=inline, title}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Buddhism|en|Buddhism|WD=Q748|lt=Buddhism|1=Buddhism}} | rite = {{ill|Rinzai school|en|Rinzai school|WD=Q639625|lt=Rinzai school|1=Rinzai school}} | deity = {{ill|Ākāśagarbha|en|Ākāśagarbha|WD=Q416174|lt=Kokūzō Bosatsu|1=Ākāśagarbha}} | country = {{flagicon|Japan}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | functional_status = | website = | founded_by =c. {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|WD=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei|1=Emperor Kinmei}} | year_completed = c.mid-6th century | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
{{nihongo| '''Kongōshō-ji'''|金剛證寺|}} is a {{ill|Rinzai school|en|Rinzai school|WD=Q639625|lt=Rinzai school|1=Rinzai school}} {{ill|Japanese Zen|en|Japanese Zen|WD=Q189424|lt=Zen|1=Japanese Zen}} {{ill|Buddhist temples in Japan|en|Buddhist temples in Japan|WD=Q3141900|lt=Buddhist temple|1=Buddhist temples in Japan}} in the Asamacho neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise|1=Ise, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. Its [[honzon|main image]] is a statue of {{ill|Ākāśagarbha|en|Ākāśagarbha|WD=Q416174|lt=Kokūzō Bosatsu|1=Ākāśagarbha}}. Founded in the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}}, it has been closely associated with the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] throughout its history and contains numerous [[:en:National Treasure of Japan|National Treasures]] and {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Properties|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}. The {{ill|Sutra mound|en|Sutra mound|WD=Q7650105|lt=Sutra mound|1=Sutra mound}}s found on Mount Asama behind the temple were designated a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1936.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210420|title=本居宣長墓（樹敬寺）附 本居春庭墓|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=20 August 2020}}</ref>
==History== The early history of Kongōshō-ji is uncertain. The temple claims that it was founded by {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|WD=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei|1=Emperor Kinmei}}, who ordered the monk Kyōtai to build a chapel on this site in the middle of the 6th century. Later, {{ill|Kūkai|en|Kūkai|WD=Q222227|lt=Kūkai|1=Kūkai}} converted this chapel into a ''{{ill|Dojo|en|Dojo|WD=Q203609|lt=Dojo|1=Dojo}}'' for the teaching of {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|WD=Q550182|lt=Shingon|1=Shingon Buddhism}} esoteric Buddhism in 825 AD. As Kongōshō-ji is located to the northeast of the Ise Grand Shrine, it was regarded as the protector of the spiritually vulnerable northeastern direction from the shrine.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.iseshimaskyline.com/kongoushouji.htm |title=Kongōshō-ji |publisher=Mie Prefecture Tourism Development Company |accessdate=10 April 2011 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110122100632/http://www.iseshimaskyline.com/kongoushouji.htm |archive-date=22 January 2011 |url-status=dead }}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bes.or.jp/english/parks/iseshima.html |title=Ise-Shima National Park |publisher=National Parks Foundation |accessdate=11 April 2011 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110611210856/http://www.bes.or.jp/english/parks/iseshima.html |archive-date=11 June 2011 |url-status=dead }}</ref> Furthermore, under the ''[[Shinbutsu-shūgō]]'' doctrines of medieval Japan, Kokūzō Bosatsu was regarded as an {{ill|Avatar|en|Avatar|WD=Q83090|lt=Avatar|1=Avatar}} of [[Amaterasu]], the principal ''[[kami]]'' of the Ise Grand Shrine, and thus the temple was regarded as an inner sanctum of that shrine. It attracted many pilgrims and grew to be the largest temple in {{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|WD=Q864166|lt=Ise|1=Ise Province}} and {{ill|Shima Province|en|Shima Province|WD=Q1053722|lt=Shima Province|1=Shima Province}}s. The temple went into slow decline in the later {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian|1=Heian period}} and {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}s, until the priest Togaku Buniku of [[Kenchō-ji]] in {{ill|Kamakura|en|Kamakura|WD=Q200267|lt=Kamakura|1=Kamakura}} converted it into a Rinzai Zen temple in 1392. The temple was patronized by the naval commander {{ill|Kuki Yoshitaka|en|Kuki Yoshitaka|WD=Q921113|lt=Kuki Yoshitaka|1=Kuki Yoshitaka}} during the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}}, becoming the ''{{ill|Bodaiji|en|Bodaiji|WD=Q1084697|lt=Bodaiji|1=Bodaiji}}'' of the Kuki clan. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, the temple was supported by the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. Its {{ill|Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)|en|Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)|WD=Q17051413|lt=Hondō|1=Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)}} was built by {{ill|Ikeda Terumasa|en|Ikeda Terumasa|WD=Q1385927|lt=Ikeda Terumasa|1=Ikeda Terumasa}} in 1609, and was restored after a fire in 1701 by Keishōin, the mother of {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Shogun|1=Shogun}} [[Tokugawa Tsunayoshi]]. It is designated a National Important Cultural Property.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Following the [[Meiji Restoration|Meiji restoration]], the temple continued to draw pilgrims and tourists, especially after the construction of a cable car in 1925 to facilitate the steep climb up the mountain. However, the cable car was scrapped for metal during {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War|1=Pacific War}}, and visitors declined in numbers, especially after damage sustained in the {{ill|Typhoon Vera|en|Typhoon Vera|WD=Q1060156|lt=Isewan Typhoon|1=Typhoon Vera}} of 1959. This situation changed after the opening of the Iseshima Skyline highway in 1964.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery widths="150" heights="110"> file:Kongoshoji(Mie)_06.JPG|Hondō file:Kongōshō-ji (Niomon).jpg|Niō-mon (rebuilt in 1979) file:Kongoshoji-sotoba_03.JPG|Oku-no-in file:Asama-yama(Mie)kyou-duka_01.JPG|Asamayama Sutra Mounds </gallery>
==Sutra mounds== About 40 {{ill|Sutra mound|en|Sutra mound|WD=Q7650105|lt=Sutra mound|1=Sutra mound}}s have been confirmed at the summit of Mount Asakuma. The existence of the mounds was known since the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}, but more mounds were found after damage caused by the 1959 Isewan Typhoon. The mounds consist of a chamber built from mountain stones, and many were found to contain artifacts from end of the Heian period. In 1966, the area was designated as a National Historic Site, and many of the excavated items were collectively designated as a National Treasure in 1963. Many of these items are exhibited in the temple museum.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/863 |title=Database of Registered National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=10 April 2011}}</ref> These include bronze sutra containers, mirrors with [[Amitābha|Amida]] and the Amida triad, and ceramics.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://sips03.narahaku.go.jp/exhib/2007toku/shinbutsu/shinbutsu-2_01_e.htm |title=Exhibition - list of entries (No.50) |publisher=[[Nara National Museum]] |accessdate=10 April 2011 |url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110719232853/http://sips03.narahaku.go.jp/exhib/2007toku/shinbutsu/shinbutsu-2_01_e.htm |archivedate=19 July 2011 }}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=9&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000483 |title=Artifacts from the Kyōgamine sutra mound |publisher={{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}} |accessdate=10 April 2011 |url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110911113647/http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=9&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000483 |archivedate=11 September 2011 }}</ref> The area of funerary monuments where these items were found has been designated an {{ill|List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments|en|List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments|WD=Q20982902|lt=Historic Site|1=List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments}}.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1525 |title=Database of Registered National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=10 April 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=4&spetype=11&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000489 |title=Mount Asama Kyōzukagun |publisher={{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}} |accessdate=10 April 2011 |url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110911113729/http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=4&spetype=11&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000489 |archivedate=11 September 2011 }}</ref>
==Buildings== The '''Hondō''' is a seven by six {{ill|Ken (unit)|en|Ken (unit)|WD=Q3195007|lt=bay|1=Ken (unit)}}, ''[[:en:Yosemune-zukuri|Yosemune-zukuri]]'' building with a ''[[hinoki]]'' bark roof. Built in 1609, it is an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1295 |title=Database of Registered National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=10 April 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=1&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000453 |title=Kongōshō-ji Hondō |publisher={{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}} |accessdate=10 April 2011 |url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110911113234/http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=1&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000453 |archivedate=11 September 2011 }}</ref>
==Cultural Properties== *{{ill|Kuki Yoshitaka|en|Kuki Yoshitaka|WD=Q921113|lt=Kuki Yoshitaka|1=Kuki Yoshitaka}} portrait (1607) ({{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}).<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/1205 |title=Database of Registered National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=10 April 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&speclass=1&spetype=2&dettype=2&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000455 |title=Portrait of Kuki Yoshitaka |publisher={{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}} |accessdate=10 April 2011 |url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110911113843/http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&speclass=1&spetype=2&dettype=2&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000455 |archivedate=11 September 2011 }}</ref>
*Uhōdōji statue ({{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}) (Important Cultural Property).<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/3099 |title=Database of Registered National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=10 April 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=3&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000457 |title=Uhōdōji |publisher={{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}} |accessdate=10 April 2011 |url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110911113342/http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=3&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000457 |archivedate=11 September 2011 }}</ref>
*[[:en:Jizō Bosatsu|Jizō Bosatsu]] statue ({{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}) (Important Cultural Property).<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011366 |title=Database of Registered National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=10 April 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=3&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000941 |title=Jizō Bosatsu |publisher={{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}} |accessdate=10 April 2011 |url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110911113421/http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=3&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000941 |archivedate=11 September 2011 }}</ref>
* [[Tachi]] (Heian period) (Important Cultural Property).<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6240 |title=Database of Registered National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=10 April 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=4&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000462 |title=Tachi |publisher={{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}} |accessdate=10 April 2011 |url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110911113539/http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=4&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000462 |archivedate=11 September 2011 }}</ref>
*{{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}} with twin phoenixes (Heian period) (Important Cultural Property).<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/5272 |title=Database of Registered National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=10 April 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=4&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000461 |title=Bronze mirror with twin phoenixes |publisher={{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}} |accessdate=10 April 2011 |url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110911113439/http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=4&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000461 |archivedate=11 September 2011 }}</ref>
* Artifacts from the Asamayama sutra mounds (1159–1173) ({{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|WD=Q1139795|lt=National Treasure|1=National Treasure (Japan)}}).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[arz:كونجوشو جى]][[co:commons:Category:Kongōshō-ji]][[en:Kongōshō-ji]][[fr:Kongōshō-ji]][[ja:金剛證寺]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[arz:كونجوشو جى]][[co:commons:Category:Kongōshō-ji]][[en:Kongōshō-ji]][[fr:Kongōshō-ji]][[ja:金剛證寺]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[arz:كونجوشو جى]][[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Kongōshō-ji]][[en:Kongōshō-ji]][[fr:Kongōshō-ji]][[ja:金剛證寺]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Kongoshoji(Mie) 07.JPG|religious_affiliation={{ill|Buddhism|qid=Q748|1=Buddhism}}|prefecture={{ill|Ise|qid=Q328067|1=Ise, Mie}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.4575|N|136.78555556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|founded_by={{ill|Kūkai|qid=Q222227|1=Kūkai}}|deity={{ill|Ākāśagarbha|qid=Q416174|1=Ākāśagarbha}}|part_of={{ill|Q11379122|qid=Q11379122|1=Q11379122}}}}
{{Short description|Rinzai Zen Buddhist temple, Japan}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kongōshō-ji | native_name = 金剛證寺 | image = Kongoshoji(Mie) 06.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kongōshō-ji Main Hall | map_type = Japan Mie Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kongōshō-ji | location = 548 Dake, Asamacho Ise-shi, Mie-ken | coordinates = {{coord|34|27|27|N|136|47|08|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=inline, title}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Buddhism|en|Buddhism|qid=Q748|lt=Buddhism|1=Buddhism}} | rite = {{ill|Rinzai school|en|Rinzai school|qid=Q639625|lt=Rinzai school|1=Rinzai school}} | deity = {{ill|Ākāśagarbha|en|Ākāśagarbha|qid=Q416174|lt=Kokūzō Bosatsu|1=Ākāśagarbha}} | country = {{flagicon|Japan}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | functional_status = | website = | founded_by =c. {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|qid=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei|1=Emperor Kinmei}} | year_completed = c.mid-6th century | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
{{nihongo| '''Kongōshō-ji'''|金剛證寺|}} is a {{ill|Rinzai school|en|Rinzai school|qid=Q639625|lt=Rinzai school|1=Rinzai school}} {{ill|Japanese Zen|en|Japanese Zen|qid=Q189424|lt=Zen|1=Japanese Zen}} {{ill|Buddhist temples in Japan|en|Buddhist temples in Japan|qid=Q3141900|lt=Buddhist temple|1=Buddhist temples in Japan}} in the Asamacho neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|qid=Q328067|lt=Ise|1=Ise, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|qid=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. Its [[honzon|main image]] is a statue of {{ill|Ākāśagarbha|en|Ākāśagarbha|qid=Q416174|lt=Kokūzō Bosatsu|1=Ākāśagarbha}}. Founded in the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|qid=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}}, it has been closely associated with the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] throughout its history and contains numerous [[:en:National Treasure of Japan|National Treasures]] and {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Properties|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}. The {{ill|Sutra mound|en|Sutra mound|qid=Q7650105|lt=Sutra mound|1=Sutra mound}}s found on Mount Asama behind the temple were designated a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1936.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210420|title=本居宣長墓（樹敬寺）附 本居春庭墓|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=20 August 2020}}</ref>
==History== The early history of Kongōshō-ji is uncertain. The temple claims that it was founded by {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|qid=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei|1=Emperor Kinmei}}, who ordered the monk Kyōtai to build a chapel on this site in the middle of the 6th century. Later, {{ill|Kūkai|en|Kūkai|qid=Q222227|lt=Kūkai|1=Kūkai}} converted this chapel into a ''{{ill|Dojo|en|Dojo|qid=Q203609|lt=Dojo|1=Dojo}}'' for the teaching of {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|qid=Q550182|lt=Shingon|1=Shingon Buddhism}} esoteric Buddhism in 825 AD. As Kongōshō-ji is located to the northeast of the Ise Grand Shrine, it was regarded as the protector of the spiritually vulnerable northeastern direction from the shrine.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.iseshimaskyline.com/kongoushouji.htm |title=Kongōshō-ji |publisher=Mie Prefecture Tourism Development Company |accessdate=10 April 2011 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110122100632/http://www.iseshimaskyline.com/kongoushouji.htm |archive-date=22 January 2011 |url-status=dead }}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bes.or.jp/english/parks/iseshima.html |title=Ise-Shima National Park |publisher=National Parks Foundation |accessdate=11 April 2011 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110611210856/http://www.bes.or.jp/english/parks/iseshima.html |archive-date=11 June 2011 |url-status=dead }}</ref> Furthermore, under the ''[[Shinbutsu-shūgō]]'' doctrines of medieval Japan, Kokūzō Bosatsu was regarded as an {{ill|Avatar|en|Avatar|qid=Q83090|lt=Avatar|1=Avatar}} of [[Amaterasu]], the principal ''[[kami]]'' of the Ise Grand Shrine, and thus the temple was regarded as an inner sanctum of that shrine. It attracted many pilgrims and grew to be the largest temple in {{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|qid=Q864166|lt=Ise|1=Ise Province}} and {{ill|Shima Province|en|Shima Province|qid=Q1053722|lt=Shima Province|1=Shima Province}}s. The temple went into slow decline in the later {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian|1=Heian period}} and {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|qid=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}s, until the priest Togaku Buniku of [[Kenchō-ji]] in {{ill|Kamakura|en|Kamakura|qid=Q200267|lt=Kamakura|1=Kamakura}} converted it into a Rinzai Zen temple in 1392. The temple was patronized by the naval commander {{ill|Kuki Yoshitaka|en|Kuki Yoshitaka|qid=Q921113|lt=Kuki Yoshitaka|1=Kuki Yoshitaka}} during the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|qid=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}}, becoming the ''{{ill|Bodaiji|en|Bodaiji|qid=Q1084697|lt=Bodaiji|1=Bodaiji}}'' of the Kuki clan. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, the temple was supported by the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. Its {{ill|Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)|en|Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)|qid=Q17051413|lt=Hondō|1=Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)}} was built by {{ill|Ikeda Terumasa|en|Ikeda Terumasa|qid=Q1385927|lt=Ikeda Terumasa|1=Ikeda Terumasa}} in 1609, and was restored after a fire in 1701 by Keishōin, the mother of {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|qid=Q131767|lt=Shogun|1=Shogun}} [[Tokugawa Tsunayoshi]]. It is designated a National Important Cultural Property.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Following the [[Meiji Restoration|Meiji restoration]], the temple continued to draw pilgrims and tourists, especially after the construction of a cable car in 1925 to facilitate the steep climb up the mountain. However, the cable car was scrapped for metal during {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|qid=Q184425|lt=Pacific War|1=Pacific War}}, and visitors declined in numbers, especially after damage sustained in the {{ill|Typhoon Vera|en|Typhoon Vera|qid=Q1060156|lt=Isewan Typhoon|1=Typhoon Vera}} of 1959. This situation changed after the opening of the Iseshima Skyline highway in 1964.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery widths="150" heights="110"> file:Kongoshoji(Mie)_06.JPG|Hondō file:Kongōshō-ji (Niomon).jpg|Niō-mon (rebuilt in 1979) file:Kongoshoji-sotoba_03.JPG|Oku-no-in file:Asama-yama(Mie)kyou-duka_01.JPG|Asamayama Sutra Mounds </gallery>
==Sutra mounds== About 40 {{ill|Sutra mound|en|Sutra mound|qid=Q7650105|lt=Sutra mound|1=Sutra mound}}s have been confirmed at the summit of Mount Asakuma. The existence of the mounds was known since the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}, but more mounds were found after damage caused by the 1959 Isewan Typhoon. The mounds consist of a chamber built from mountain stones, and many were found to contain artifacts from end of the Heian period. In 1966, the area was designated as a National Historic Site, and many of the excavated items were collectively designated as a National Treasure in 1963. Many of these items are exhibited in the temple museum.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/863 |title=Database of Registered National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=10 April 2011}}</ref> These include bronze sutra containers, mirrors with [[Amitābha|Amida]] and the Amida triad, and ceramics.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://sips03.narahaku.go.jp/exhib/2007toku/shinbutsu/shinbutsu-2_01_e.htm |title=Exhibition - list of entries (No.50) |publisher=[[Nara National Museum]] |accessdate=10 April 2011 |url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110719232853/http://sips03.narahaku.go.jp/exhib/2007toku/shinbutsu/shinbutsu-2_01_e.htm |archivedate=19 July 2011 }}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=9&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000483 |title=Artifacts from the Kyōgamine sutra mound |publisher={{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|qid=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}} |accessdate=10 April 2011 |url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110911113647/http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=9&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000483 |archivedate=11 September 2011 }}</ref> The area of funerary monuments where these items were found has been designated an {{ill|List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments|en|List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments|qid=Q20982902|lt=Historic Site|1=List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments}}.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1525 |title=Database of Registered National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=10 April 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=4&spetype=11&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000489 |title=Mount Asama Kyōzukagun |publisher={{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|qid=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}} |accessdate=10 April 2011 |url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110911113729/http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=4&spetype=11&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000489 |archivedate=11 September 2011 }}</ref>
==Buildings== The '''Hondō''' is a seven by six {{ill|Ken (unit)|en|Ken (unit)|qid=Q3195007|lt=bay|1=Ken (unit)}}, ''[[:en:Yosemune-zukuri|Yosemune-zukuri]]'' building with a ''[[hinoki]]'' bark roof. Built in 1609, it is an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1295 |title=Database of Registered National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=10 April 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=1&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000453 |title=Kongōshō-ji Hondō |publisher={{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|qid=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}} |accessdate=10 April 2011 |url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110911113234/http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=1&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000453 |archivedate=11 September 2011 }}</ref>
==Cultural Properties== *{{ill|Kuki Yoshitaka|en|Kuki Yoshitaka|qid=Q921113|lt=Kuki Yoshitaka|1=Kuki Yoshitaka}} portrait (1607) ({{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}).<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/1205 |title=Database of Registered National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=10 April 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&speclass=1&spetype=2&dettype=2&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000455 |title=Portrait of Kuki Yoshitaka |publisher={{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|qid=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}} |accessdate=10 April 2011 |url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110911113843/http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&speclass=1&spetype=2&dettype=2&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000455 |archivedate=11 September 2011 }}</ref>
*Uhōdōji statue ({{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}) (Important Cultural Property).<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/3099 |title=Database of Registered National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=10 April 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=3&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000457 |title=Uhōdōji |publisher={{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|qid=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}} |accessdate=10 April 2011 |url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110911113342/http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=3&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000457 |archivedate=11 September 2011 }}</ref>
*[[:en:Jizō Bosatsu|Jizō Bosatsu]] statue ({{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|qid=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}) (Important Cultural Property).<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011366 |title=Database of Registered National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=10 April 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=3&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000941 |title=Jizō Bosatsu |publisher={{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|qid=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}} |accessdate=10 April 2011 |url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110911113421/http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=3&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000941 |archivedate=11 September 2011 }}</ref>
* [[Tachi]] (Heian period) (Important Cultural Property).<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6240 |title=Database of Registered National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=10 April 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=4&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000462 |title=Tachi |publisher={{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|qid=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}} |accessdate=10 April 2011 |url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110911113539/http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=4&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000462 |archivedate=11 September 2011 }}</ref>
*{{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|qid=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}} with twin phoenixes (Heian period) (Important Cultural Property).<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/5272 |title=Database of Registered National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=10 April 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=4&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000461 |title=Bronze mirror with twin phoenixes |publisher={{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|qid=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}} |accessdate=10 April 2011 |url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110911113439/http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=4&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000461 |archivedate=11 September 2011 }}</ref>
* Artifacts from the Asamayama sutra mounds (1159–1173) ({{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|qid=Q1139795|lt=National Treasure|1=National Treasure (Japan)}}).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[arz:كونجوشو جى]][[co:commons:Category:Kongōshō-ji]][[en:Kongōshō-ji]][[fr:Kongōshō-ji]][[ja:金剛證寺]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[arz:كونجوشو جى]][[co:commons:Category:Kongōshō-ji]][[en:Kongōshō-ji]][[fr:Kongōshō-ji]][[ja:金剛證寺]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[arz:كونجوشو جى]][[en:Kongōshō-ji]][[fr:Kongōshō-ji]][[ja:金剛證寺]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Kongoshoji(Mie) 07.JPG|religious_affiliation={{ill|Buddhism|qid=Q748|1=Buddhism}}|prefecture={{ill|Ise|qid=Q328067|1=Ise, Mie}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.4575|N|136.78555556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|founded_by={{ill|Kūkai|qid=Q222227|1=Kūkai}}|deity={{ill|Ākāśagarbha|qid=Q416174|1=Ākāśagarbha}}|part_of={{ill|Q11379122|qid=Q11379122|1=Q11379122}}}}
{{Short description|Rinzai Zen Buddhist temple, Japan}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kongōshō-ji | native_name = 金剛證寺 | image = Kongoshoji(Mie) 06.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kongōshō-ji Main Hall | map_type = Japan Mie Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kongōshō-ji | location = 548 Dake, Asamacho Ise-shi, Mie-ken | coordinates = {{coord|34|27|27|N|136|47|08|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=inline, title}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Buddhism|en|Buddhism|qid=Q748|lt=Buddhism|1=Buddhism}} | rite = {{ill|Rinzai school|en|Rinzai school|qid=Q639625|lt=Rinzai school|1=Rinzai school}} | deity = {{ill|Ākāśagarbha|en|Ākāśagarbha|qid=Q416174|lt=Kokūzō Bosatsu|1=Ākāśagarbha}} | country = {{flagicon|Japan}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | functional_status = | website = | founded_by =c. {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|qid=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei|1=Emperor Kinmei}} | year_completed = c.mid-6th century | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
{{nihongo| '''Kongōshō-ji'''|金剛證寺|}} is a {{ill|Rinzai school|en|Rinzai school|qid=Q639625|lt=Rinzai school|1=Rinzai school}} {{ill|Japanese Zen|en|Japanese Zen|qid=Q189424|lt=Zen|1=Japanese Zen}} {{ill|Buddhist temples in Japan|en|Buddhist temples in Japan|qid=Q3141900|lt=Buddhist temple|1=Buddhist temples in Japan}} in the Asamacho neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|qid=Q328067|lt=Ise|1=Ise, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|qid=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. Its [[honzon|main image]] is a statue of {{ill|Ākāśagarbha|en|Ākāśagarbha|qid=Q416174|lt=Kokūzō Bosatsu|1=Ākāśagarbha}}. Founded in the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|qid=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}}, it has been closely associated with the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] throughout its history and contains numerous [[:en:National Treasure of Japan|National Treasures]] and {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Properties|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}. The {{ill|Sutra mound|en|Sutra mound|qid=Q7650105|lt=Sutra mound|1=Sutra mound}}s found on Mount Asama behind the temple were designated a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1936.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210420|title=本居宣長墓（樹敬寺）附 本居春庭墓|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=20 August 2020}}</ref>
==History== The early history of Kongōshō-ji is uncertain. The temple claims that it was founded by {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|qid=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei|1=Emperor Kinmei}}, who ordered the monk Kyōtai to build a chapel on this site in the middle of the 6th century. Later, {{ill|Kūkai|en|Kūkai|qid=Q222227|lt=Kūkai|1=Kūkai}} converted this chapel into a ''{{ill|Dojo|en|Dojo|qid=Q203609|lt=Dojo|1=Dojo}}'' for the teaching of {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|qid=Q550182|lt=Shingon|1=Shingon Buddhism}} esoteric Buddhism in 825 AD. As Kongōshō-ji is located to the northeast of the Ise Grand Shrine, it was regarded as the protector of the spiritually vulnerable northeastern direction from the shrine.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.iseshimaskyline.com/kongoushouji.htm |title=Kongōshō-ji |publisher=Mie Prefecture Tourism Development Company |accessdate=10 April 2011 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110122100632/http://www.iseshimaskyline.com/kongoushouji.htm |archive-date=22 January 2011 |url-status=dead }}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bes.or.jp/english/parks/iseshima.html |title=Ise-Shima National Park |publisher=National Parks Foundation |accessdate=11 April 2011 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110611210856/http://www.bes.or.jp/english/parks/iseshima.html |archive-date=11 June 2011 |url-status=dead }}</ref> Furthermore, under the ''[[Shinbutsu-shūgō]]'' doctrines of medieval Japan, Kokūzō Bosatsu was regarded as an {{ill|Avatar|en|Avatar|qid=Q83090|lt=Avatar|1=Avatar}} of [[Amaterasu]], the principal ''[[kami]]'' of the Ise Grand Shrine, and thus the temple was regarded as an inner sanctum of that shrine. It attracted many pilgrims and grew to be the largest temple in {{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|qid=Q864166|lt=Ise|1=Ise Province}} and {{ill|Shima Province|en|Shima Province|qid=Q1053722|lt=Shima Province|1=Shima Province}}s. The temple went into slow decline in the later {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian|1=Heian period}} and {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|qid=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}s, until the priest Togaku Buniku of [[Kenchō-ji]] in {{ill|Kamakura|en|Kamakura|qid=Q200267|lt=Kamakura|1=Kamakura}} converted it into a Rinzai Zen temple in 1392. The temple was patronized by the naval commander {{ill|Kuki Yoshitaka|en|Kuki Yoshitaka|qid=Q921113|lt=Kuki Yoshitaka|1=Kuki Yoshitaka}} during the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|qid=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}}, becoming the ''{{ill|Bodaiji|en|Bodaiji|qid=Q1084697|lt=Bodaiji|1=Bodaiji}}'' of the Kuki clan. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, the temple was supported by the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. Its {{ill|Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)|en|Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)|qid=Q17051413|lt=Hondō|1=Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)}} was built by {{ill|Ikeda Terumasa|en|Ikeda Terumasa|qid=Q1385927|lt=Ikeda Terumasa|1=Ikeda Terumasa}} in 1609, and was restored after a fire in 1701 by Keishōin, the mother of {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|qid=Q131767|lt=Shogun|1=Shogun}} [[Tokugawa Tsunayoshi]]. It is designated a National Important Cultural Property.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Following the [[Meiji Restoration|Meiji restoration]], the temple continued to draw pilgrims and tourists, especially after the construction of a cable car in 1925 to facilitate the steep climb up the mountain. However, the cable car was scrapped for metal during {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|qid=Q184425|lt=Pacific War|1=Pacific War}}, and visitors declined in numbers, especially after damage sustained in the {{ill|Typhoon Vera|en|Typhoon Vera|qid=Q1060156|lt=Isewan Typhoon|1=Typhoon Vera}} of 1959. This situation changed after the opening of the Iseshima Skyline highway in 1964.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery widths="150" heights="110"> file:Kongoshoji(Mie)_06.JPG|Hondō file:Kongōshō-ji (Niomon).jpg|Niō-mon (rebuilt in 1979) file:Kongoshoji-sotoba_03.JPG|Oku-no-in file:Asama-yama(Mie)kyou-duka_01.JPG|Asamayama Sutra Mounds </gallery>
==Sutra mounds== About 40 {{ill|Sutra mound|en|Sutra mound|qid=Q7650105|lt=Sutra mound|1=Sutra mound}}s have been confirmed at the summit of Mount Asakuma. The existence of the mounds was known since the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}, but more mounds were found after damage caused by the 1959 Isewan Typhoon. The mounds consist of a chamber built from mountain stones, and many were found to contain artifacts from end of the Heian period. In 1966, the area was designated as a National Historic Site, and many of the excavated items were collectively designated as a National Treasure in 1963. Many of these items are exhibited in the temple museum.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/863 |title=Database of Registered National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=10 April 2011}}</ref> These include bronze sutra containers, mirrors with [[Amitābha|Amida]] and the Amida triad, and ceramics.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://sips03.narahaku.go.jp/exhib/2007toku/shinbutsu/shinbutsu-2_01_e.htm |title=Exhibition - list of entries (No.50) |publisher=[[Nara National Museum]] |accessdate=10 April 2011 |url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110719232853/http://sips03.narahaku.go.jp/exhib/2007toku/shinbutsu/shinbutsu-2_01_e.htm |archivedate=19 July 2011 }}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=9&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000483 |title=Artifacts from the Kyōgamine sutra mound |publisher={{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|qid=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}} |accessdate=10 April 2011 |url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110911113647/http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=9&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000483 |archivedate=11 September 2011 }}</ref> The area of funerary monuments where these items were found has been designated an {{ill|List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments|en|List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments|qid=Q20982902|lt=Historic Site|1=List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments}}.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1525 |title=Database of Registered National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=10 April 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=4&spetype=11&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000489 |title=Mount Asama Kyōzukagun |publisher={{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|qid=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}} |accessdate=10 April 2011 |url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110911113729/http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=4&spetype=11&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000489 |archivedate=11 September 2011 }}</ref>
==Buildings== The '''Hondō''' is a seven by six {{ill|Ken (unit)|en|Ken (unit)|qid=Q3195007|lt=bay|1=Ken (unit)}}, ''[[:en:Yosemune-zukuri|Yosemune-zukuri]]'' building with a ''[[hinoki]]'' bark roof. Built in 1609, it is an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1295 |title=Database of Registered National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=10 April 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=1&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000453 |title=Kongōshō-ji Hondō |publisher={{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|qid=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}} |accessdate=10 April 2011 |url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110911113234/http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?pub=1&pub=2&speclass=1&spetype=1&spetype=2&spetype=3&dettype=1&wag=7&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000453 |archivedate=11 September 2011 }}</ref>
